message:
fix(storage): reject update_issue against an existing tombstone

`SqliteStorage::update_issue` would happily apply field changes to a
row that had already been tombstoned, effectively resurrecting the
issue with whatever fields the caller passed. The deletion machinery
(`delete_issue` / tombstone propagation) was therefore reversible by
any path that called `update_issue` on a tombstoned ID — including
sync paths and edit commands that did not realize they were touching
a deleted issue.

Fix: inside the existing CONCURRENT transaction (after the row has
been loaded with `SELECT ... FOR UPDATE` semantics), check
`issue.status == Status::Tombstone` before applying any update and
return `BeadsError::Validation { field: "issue_id", reason: "cannot
update tombstone issue: <id>" }`. The check sits inside the txn so
two callers cannot race the resurrection.

Test `test_update_issue_rejects_existing_tombstone`:
- Create issue `bd-u-tomb`, then `delete_issue` it (creating the
  tombstone).
- Clear dirty tracking so the assertion after the failed update is
  unambiguous.
- Call `update_issue` with title/status changes plus all three
  `deleted_*` fields explicitly cleared (the resurrection shape).
- Assert the error is `Validation` on `issue_id` and the reason
  string mentions the tombstone ID.
- Reload the row and assert title, deleted_at, deleted_by, and
  delete_reason all still match the tombstone — the failed update
  did not leak any partial change — and that the dirty-issue set is
  still empty (no spurious dirty marker from the rejected attempt).

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
